element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic introduced immediately; players learn that limbs return if they hit but take longer if they miss
Punch fruit repeatedly to grow it and restore more health; Golden Fruits fully replenish energy (rare)
One token per level completed without losing a life; four tokens earn a Plokontinue checkpoint
Most levels require eliminating all fleas; final flea drops flag to proceed. Absent from Cotton Island, Legacy Island, and Fleapit
Serve as ammo for amulet secondary weapon; grant extra lives when collected
Amulet-powered enhanced speed attack with maximum damage; powered by collected shells
Grant movie-inspired costumes: Plocky (boxing gloves), Vigilante Plok (flamethrower), Squire Plok (blunderbuss), Cowboy Plok (guns), Rocket Plok (rocket launchers)
Certain puzzles require sacrificing a limb to activate scenery switches; some targets require specific limbs
Access via Magic Fruits; complete challenges to skip levels or boss fights
High-tech presents unlock: unicycle (water cannon), off-road truck (rocket), jetpack (lasers), motorbike (grenades), tank, helicopter (bombs), flying saucer (plasma), pogo shoes

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters (Bobbins Brothers, Rockyfella, Flea Queen)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defeating Bobbins and Rockyfella unlock Permanent Continue Positions for save functionality

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating all fleas in level and collecting flag
Finding Magic Fruits to restore health
Collecting P-L-O-K tokens for Plokontinue checkpoint
Accessing bonus areas with timed challenges
typical_tension_triggers: Limb sacrifice mechanics (permanent loss of attack limbs)
Enemy flea elimination requirements (level blocked until all defeated)
Difficult platforming sections requiring limb management
Loss of limbs forcing bounce locomotion (reduced control)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from basic limb-throwing (Cotton Island) through flea-elimination puzzles (Akrillic progression) to limb-sacrifice mechanics and vehicle combat (Fleapit). Two difficulty modes (Normal vs. Child's Play) allow tension modulation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Magic Fruits that transport to bonus areas; Golden Fruits hidden for players seeking full recovery; Presents scattered throughout stages for temporary power-ups
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Fruit inflation mechanism (punch 3+ times to burst and reveal secret bonus); specific limbs required for certain sacrifice targets

difficulty_curve.early_game: Cotton Island introduces basic limb mechanics in safe context; no mandatory flea elimination; primarily platforming and flag retrieval
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Akrillic 1-16: Progressive introduction of flea enemies (mandatory elimination), limb sacrifice puzzles, shell/amulet secondary attacks, and movie-inspired Presents; two Permanent Continue unlocks via boss defeats (Bobbins, Rockyfella)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fleapit: Vehicle-exclusive Presents with integrated weapons systems; highest-tech combat encounters; flea Queen as final challenge
notable_spikes: Bobbins Brothers boss fight (unlocks first Permanent Continue)
Introduction of limb sacrifice mechanic (level design complexity jump)
Rockyfella boss fight (unlocks second Permanent Continue)
Fleapit vehicle combat transitions (weapon-based rather than limb-based gameplay)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player searches island for Plok's stolen flag, discovering impostor flags (underpants, striped scarf) that frustrate progression; no mandatory flea elimination
Standard level structure: eliminate all fleas in stage to spawn final flea carrying flag; flag levitates to pole enabling level exit; introduces limb sacrifice switches requiring specific limbs

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Bonus area gating via fruit transport
notable_level_patterns.description: Magic Fruits scattered through levels transport players to self-contained timed challenges or shell-collection puzzles; successful completion allows skipping subsequent levels or boss fights
Final world exclusively uses vehicle Presents for combat; high-tech weapon systems (water cannons, rockets, plasma, grenades, bombs) replace limb-throwing mechanics; futuristic tileset

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Plok article)
